title: check-status drift report is command-only — no context-check-status op, unreachable from the reflecting CLI
description: The pure-read schema-drift report (checkStatus in packages/pi-context/src/index.ts, rendered by renderCheckStatus) is surfaced ONLY as the /context check-status command handler (CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS in index.ts). There is no OpDefinition for it in the op-registry, and the reflecting pi-context-cli only surfaces surface:"use" ops — so check-status is unreachable from the CLI (a human or agent driving node packages/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js cannot get the drift report). This is at odds with the CLI-parity direction (R-0003) and inconsistent with how /context status pairs with the context-status op. checkStatus is pure-read (returns CheckStatusReport, writes nothing), so it is a clean op candidate with no writer-classification or cap concerns.

evidence.reference: checkStatus(cwd): CheckStatusReport (pure-read) + the 'check-status' entry in CONTEXT_SUBCOMMANDS — command handler only
no context-check-status OpDefinition; the op-registry does not expose the drift report
useOps = ops.filter(o => o.surface === 'use') — the CLI reflects ops only, so a command-only surface is invisible to it

impact: The drift report is in-pi-only; the reflecting pi-context-cli (and orchestrator-script consumers) cannot invoke check-status, undercutting the CLI-parity direction. Same family as the FGAP-021..026 CLI-parity gaps.
proposed_resolution: Add a context-check-status OpDefinition (surface:"use", empty parameters, run(cwd) -> { json: checkStatus(cwd) } — pure-read, boundedJsonOutput-capped) so the reflecting pi-context-cli surfaces it automatically. OP-ONLY — NO orchestrator-script twin (per DEC-0011: new ops are op-only; the reflecting CLI is the shell surface; hand-writing a script twin would be a born-obsolete artifact). Pure-read, so no writer-classification/allowlist needed. Run through the canonical pipeline.
